UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. WITHIN 10 DAYS OF THE HEARING DATE, DEFENDANTS SHALL SERVE VERIFIED SUPPLEMENTAL RESPONSES AND PRODUCE RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 7 - 11, 14, 15, 24 AND 25. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE TAKEN UNDER SIBMISSION. THE MATTER HAS BEEN DELAYED DUE TO DEFENDANTS' FORMER COUNSEL AND IS NOT THE RESULT OF AN ABUSE OF DISCOVERY BY DEFENDANTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER TRACKING TENTATIVE RULING APPROVED AS TO FORM AND LEAVE SANCTIONS AMOUNT BLANK. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED (D501)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS LAURENCE FOK, KAM CHAN, SHANNON FOK'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 6, 2014 TO GIVE THE PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE PARTIES MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ALL PAPERS FILED IN SUPPORT OF AND OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MAY 23, 2014 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). ANY PARTY WHO CONTESTS A TENTATIVE RULING MUST SEND AN EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG WITH A COPY TO ALL OTHER PARTIES BY 4PM STATING, WITHOUT ARGUMENT, THE PORTION(S) OF THE TENTATIVE RULING THAT THE PARTY CONTESTS. THE SUBJECT LINE OF THE EMAIL SHALL INCLUDE THE LINE NUMBER, CASE NAME AND CASE NUMBER. JUDGE PRO TEM: ANTHONY SCHOENBERG, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED.=(302/JPT)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-11-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-10-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-08-2014 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kari Bowyer, Esq. (415-956-6488 x12) of Steven Adair MacDonald & Associates PC for Laurence Fok, Plaintiff, appears late, after case has been called.
John Chow, Esq. of Tsao-Wu, Chow & Yee (415-777-1688) for Peter Tom, Defendant.

Argued. Plaintiffs Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions is Granted in part. Within 10 days of the hearing date, Defendants shall serve verified supplemental responses and produce responsive documents to Request for Production nos. 7 - 11, 14, 15, 24 and 25. Monetary sanctions are taken under submission. The matter has been delayed due to Defendants' former counsel and is not the result of an abuse of discovery by Defendants. Prevailing party to submit order tracking tentative ruling approved as to form and leave sanctions amount blank. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported (D501)

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFFS LAURENCE FOK, KAM CHAN AND SHANNON FOK's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions - Transferred to Department 501, the Real Property Court, to be heard on the same date and time. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/MJM)

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-06-2014 Continued To Jun-20-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kari S. Bowyer, Esq. of Steven Adair MacDonald & Associates PC, Tel. 415.956.6488 x-12, for plaintiff and moving party;
John Chow, Esq. of Tsao-Wu, Chow & Yee LLP, Tel. 415.777.1688, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel did not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. Matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, PLAINTIFFS LAURENCE FOK, KAM CHAN, SHANNON FOK'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions is continued to June 20, 2014 at 9:30 am in Department 302. Judge Pro Tem shall prepare a Report and Recommendation. Judge Pro Tem: Rebecca Woodson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Continued To Jun-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFFs LAURENCE FOK, KAM CHAN, SHANNON FOK's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions is Continued to June 6, 2014 to give the parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The parties must deliver courtesy copies of all papers filed in support of and opposition to the motion to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than May 23, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Judge Pro Tem: Anthony Schoenberg, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported.=(302/JPT)
